# Rosalind Problem: Construct the Graph of a Spectrum

## Problem Description
Given a collection of integers representing a spectrum, construct the graph of the spectrum where vertices are the integers and edges represent differences between adjacent vertices.

## Solution

```go
package main

import (
    "fmt"
    "sort"
    "strconv"
    "strings"
)

// constructGraph constructs the graph of a spectrum
func constructGraph(spectrum []int) []string {
    // Sort the spectrum
    sort.Ints(spectrum)
    
    // Create edges based on differences
    var edges []string
    
    // For each pair of adjacent elements, create an edge
    for i := 0; i < len(spectrum)-1; i++ {
        for j := i + 1; j < len(spectrum); j++ {
            diff := spectrum[j] - spectrum[i]
            edge := fmt.Sprintf("%d->%d:%d", spectrum[i], spectrum[j], diff)
            edges = append(edges, edge)
        }
    }
    
    return edges
}

// Alternative approach - more efficient for the specific problem
func constructGraphEfficient(spectrum []int) []string {
    // Sort the spectrum
    sort.Ints(spectrum)
    
    // Create edges for consecutive elements
    var edges []string
    
    for i := 0; i < len(spectrum)-1; i++ {
        diff := spectrum[i+1] - spectrum[i]
        edge := fmt.Sprintf("%d->%d:%d", spectrum[i], spectrum[i+1], diff)
        edges = append(edges, edge)
    }
    
    return edges
}

// Function to parse input from string
func parseSpectrum(input string) []int {
    parts := strings.Fields(input)
    var spectrum []int
    
    for _, part := range parts {
        if num, err := strconv.Atoi(part); err == nil {
            spectrum = append(spectrum, num)
        }
    }
    
    return spectrum
}

func main() {
    // Example input from Rosalind
    input := "57 82 101 134 168 187 221 257 292 326 350 387 422 456 487 522 557 592 627 662 697 732 767 802 837 872 907 942 977 1012 1047 1082 1117 1152 1187 1222 1257 1292 1327 1362 1397 1432 1467
